our_state.method: SA + positional + DEDR + Elamite + Sanskrit + CISI cross-validation

fuls.contribution: ICIT corpus (4537 objects), positional NWSP method (2013/2023), sign frequency analysis. Published the foundational positional method we use.
fuls.note: Fuls provides the METHOD and DATA but not phonetic readings. Our pipeline extends his positional work with SA + PDr phonotactics.

rao_ucsd.contribution: Entropy analysis (2009) showing Indus is linguistic. Zipf/positional statistics. No sign readings proposed.

parpola.contribution: 1994/2010 Dravidian rebus hypothesis. ~30 sign readings proposed. Iconographic anchors (fish=mīn, etc.). Our starting point.
parpola.note: Parpola proposed ~30 readings but never achieved quantitative validation or corpus-wide coverage. No SA, no statistical significance tests.

revesz_daggumati.contribution: 2021 allograph detection method. We implemented this in Phase-252 (56 HIGH upgrades). No full decipherment attempted.

nair_2026.contribution: 2026 synthetic-baseline scorecard confirms Indus is STRONGLY_LINGUISTIC using same metrics as our pipeline. Supports but doesn't extend our work.

farmer_sproat_witzel.contribution: 2004 non-linguistic hypothesis. Refuted by our E28 falsification (H1=5.384 bits >> metrological max), Nair 2026, and 41 evidence items.
farmer_sproat_witzel.note: Their hypothesis is formally falsified by multiple independent tests.

analysis.verdict: NO ONE has achieved comparable coverage. Our pipeline is the first to: (1) assign readings to all 413 distinct signs, (2) achieve 100% token coverage, (3) validate with 41 independent evidence items, (4) cross-validate on CISI corpus, (5) achieve 92.5% HIGH confidence. Parpola's ~30 readings are our starting point; Fuls' positional method is our foundation; but the quantitative decipherment pipeline with SA + DEDR + Elamite/Sanskrit triple corroboration is novel to this project.
icit_access_paths: 1. Contact Dr. Andreas Fuls directly (FU Berlin) — creator of ICIT
2. Check if ICIT is bundled with Fuls 2023 publication supplementary
3. Wells 2015 'Archaeology and Epigraphy of Indus Writing' may have digitized subset
5. Holdatllc GitHub — our primary corpus source (1670 seals)
